description: Add connection resilience to the Ralph loop: capture stderr, classify errors (auth/network/rate-limit), retry with exponential backoff for transient errors, auto-pause on persistent failures, and resume from where it stopped.

userStories.title: Capture stderr from spawned Claude Code processes
userStories.description: Modify spawnProcess() in src/core/runner.js to capture stderr output from spawned Claude Code processes instead of inheriting it. Store stderr in the result object alongside stdout, exit code, and killed status. This is essential for error classification. Change stdio from ['pipe', 'pipe', 'inherit'] to ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'] and collect stderr chunks. The result object should have a new 'stderr' field with the full stderr output as a string. Also update spawnAgent() to pass stderr through to the caller. Make sure normal stderr output is still logged to console.error in real-time so the user can see progress, but also accumulated for later analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnProcess() captures stderr into result.stderr string
stderr is still printed to console.error in real-time
result object has {output, stderr, code, killed} shape
Existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Error classification module
userStories.description: Create a new function classifyError(exitCode, stderr) in src/core/runner.js that analyzes the exit code and stderr output to classify the error type. Return an object with {type, retryable, message}. Error types: 'auth' (401/403/unauthorized/forbidden/authentication in stderr — NOT retryable), 'network' (ECONNREFUSED/ETIMEDOUT/ENOTFOUND/network/socket/DNS in stderr — retryable), 'rate_limit' (429/rate.limit/too.many.requests/throttl in stderr — retryable), 'timeout' (exit code 124 or timeout in stderr — retryable), 'killed' (killed flag true — NOT retryable), 'unknown' (anything else — retryable for first 2 attempts then not). Export the function so it can be used by the run command. Add unit tests in src/core/runner.test.js.
acceptanceCriteria: classifyError() returns {type, retryable, message} object
Correctly classifies auth errors (401, 403, unauthorized) as non-retryable
Correctly classifies network errors (ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT) as retryable
Correctly classifies rate limit errors (429, too many requests) as retryable
Correctly classifies timeout (exit code 124) as retryable
Unit tests cover all error types in src/core/runner.test.js

userStories.title: Retry with exponential backoff for transient errors
userStories.description: Modify the main loop in src/commands/run.js to retry failed stories when classifyError() returns retryable=true. Implement exponential backoff: wait 5s, 15s, 45s between retries (base 5s, multiplier 3x). Maximum 3 retry attempts per story. Track retry count per story. Log each retry attempt with the error type, attempt number, and wait time. If all retries exhausted for a story, mark it as failed and move to next story. Import classifyError from runner.js. The retry logic should be in the main iteration loop after checking the result code. Between retries, log a countdown message every 5 seconds so the user knows the loop is waiting, not frozen.
acceptanceCriteria: Failed stories with retryable errors are retried up to 3 times
Backoff follows exponential pattern: 5s, 15s, 45s
Each retry logs: error type, attempt number, wait duration
After 3 failed retries, story is skipped and loop continues to next
Non-retryable errors skip retry and move to next story immediately
Countdown messages logged every 5s during backoff wait

userStories.title: Auto-pause loop on persistent auth or network failures
userStories.description: Add logic to src/commands/run.js that detects persistent failures and auto-pauses the loop instead of burning through all remaining stories. Track consecutive failures across stories (not just retries within one story). If 3 consecutive stories fail with the same non-retryable error type (like 'auth'), OR if 5 consecutive stories fail with any error type, pause the loop. When pausing: write 'paused' to .ralph/status.txt with the reason, log a clear message explaining why the loop paused and what the user should do (e.g., 'Loop paused: authentication failed 3 times consecutively. Check your API key and run ralph run to resume.'), release the lock file, and exit with code 2 (distinct from 0=success, 1=error). Also add a new function setPauseState(reason) and getPauseState() in src/core/config.js that writes/reads a .ralph/pause.json file with {reason, pausedAt, lastStoryId, consecutiveFailures}.
acceptanceCriteria: Loop pauses after 3 consecutive same-type non-retryable failures
Loop pauses after 5 consecutive failures of any type
Pause state written to .ralph/pause.json with reason and context
Status file updated to 'paused: <reason>'
Lock file released on pause
Exit code 2 on pause (distinct from 0 and 1)
Clear user-facing message logged explaining pause reason and recovery steps

userStories.title: Resume from pause state on ralph run
userStories.description: Modify src/commands/run.js so that when ralph run starts, it checks for .ralph/pause.json via getPauseState(). If a pause state exists, log a message like 'Resuming from pause (reason: auth failure). Last story: RES-003.' and clear the pause state file before starting the loop. The loop should start from the story that was running when it paused (using lastStoryId from pause.json to find the right story in prd.json). Also add a ralph status enhancement: when there is a pause.json, ralph status should show the pause reason and when it was paused. Update src/commands/status.js to read and display pause state. Delete the pause.json file after successfully resuming.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph run detects existing pause.json and logs resume message
Loop resumes from the story that was active when paused
pause.json is deleted after successful resume
ralph status shows pause state when paused (reason, time, last story)
If no pause.json exists, ralph run starts normally (no behavior change)
